agitation.note: Constant agitation at 27C/80F (3min), intermittent at 24C/75F (4min), minimal at 21C/70F (6min); times are minimum. Constant = continuous inversions; Intermittent = 30s constant then 10s every min; Minimal = 10s gentle then 5s every min.

notes: Monobath (develop+fix in one). Cubic-grain emulsion; native ISO. Times are minimum for self-completion; extending time does not increase development.

agitation.note: Constant agitation at 27C/80F (3min), intermittent at 24C/75F (4min), minimal at 21C/70F (6min); times are minimum.

notes: Monobath (develop+fix in one). Cubic-grain; native ISO 400. Standard Df96 temperature/time scheme.

agitation.note: Constant agitation at 27C/80F (3min), intermittent at 24C/75F (4min), minimal at 21C/70F (6min); times are minimum.

notes: Monobath (develop+fix in one). Cubic-grain; native ISO 400.

agitation.note: Constant agitation at 27C/80F (3min), intermittent at 24C/75F (4min), minimal at 21C/70F (6min); times are minimum.

notes: Monobath (develop+fix in one). Cubic-grain; native ISO 125.

agitation.note: Constant agitation at 27C/80F, intermittent at 24C/75F, minimal at 21C/70F; times are minimum.

notes: Monobath (develop+fix in one). Tabular-grain film with color dye tech: requires DOUBLE the label processing time to clear pink/purple dyes. Times shown are 2x the base Df96 scheme.

agitation.note: Constant agitation at 27C/80F, intermittent at 24C/75F, minimal at 21C/70F; times are minimum.

notes: Monobath (develop+fix in one). Tabular-grain with color dye tech: requires DOUBLE processing time (2x base Df96 scheme) to clear dyes.

agitation.note: Constant agitation at 27C/80F, intermittent at 24C/75F, minimal at 21C/70F; times are minimum.

notes: Monobath (develop+fix in one). Tabular-grain with color dye tech: requires DOUBLE processing time (2x base Df96 scheme).

agitation.note: Constant agitation at 27C/80F, intermittent at 24C/75F, minimal at 21C/70F; times are minimum.

notes: Monobath (develop+fix in one). Tabular-grain with color dye tech: requires DOUBLE processing time (2x base Df96 scheme).

agitation.note: Constant agitation at 27C/80F (3min), intermittent at 24C/75F (4min), minimal at 21C/70F (6min); times are minimum.

notes: Monobath (develop+fix in one). Cubic-grain; native ISO 100.

agitation.note: Constant agitation at 27C/80F (3min), intermittent at 24C/75F (4min), minimal at 21C/70F (6min); times are minimum.

notes: Monobath (develop+fix in one). Native ISO 200. (Fomapan 200 has some tabular character but CineStill lists it in the standard/normal group.)

agitation.note: Constant agitation at 27C/80F (3min), intermittent at 24C/75F (4min), minimal at 21C/70F (6min); times are minimum.

notes: Monobath (develop+fix in one). Cubic-grain; native ISO 400.

agitation.note: Intermittent agitation at 24C/75F; time is minimum (2x base for tabular dye clearing).

notes: Monobath (develop+fix in one). High-speed tabular film processed at native EI 1600 per label; requires double processing time to clear dyes. Can be pushed to 3200 by adding 10F/6C.

agitation.note: Developer step. Continuous for first 10 sec., then 4 inversion cycles every 30 sec. When no temperature-control bath, add +2F/1C to the developer before processing.

notes: C-41 developer step (Step 2). Recommended time/temp for optimal results: 3.5 min at 102F/39C. Followed by Blix 8min, wash, optional stabilizer. Full 2-bath Cs41 Color Simplified kit.

asset.title: CineStill Any C-41 color negative film processing data
CineStill Any C-41 color negative film processing data

agitation.note: Developer step at variable temperatures. Continuous first 10 sec., then 4 inversion cycles every 30 sec.

notes: Cs41 variable-temperature developer chart (normal development). 80F/27C=21min, 85F/29.5C=13min, 90F/32C=8.5min, 95F/35C=5.75min, 102F/39C=3.5min. Lower temps reduce contrast/color separation.

asset.title: CineStill Any C-41 color negative film processing data
CineStill Any C-41 color negative film processing data

agitation.note: E-6 first developer step. Only the 1st Developer is time/temperature critical; other baths process to completion as they cool. When no temp-control bath, preheat 1st developer +2F warmer.

notes: Cs6 Creative Slide 3-bath E-6 process. D6 neutral-tone 5500K 1st developer, normal development: 1+1 dilution ~6min at 104F/40C. Push +1: stock 6min or 1+1 8min; Push +2: stock 8min. (Note: product summary line separately states 1+1 2min @104F, but the detailed instructions block specifies 6min for normal development.)
